original_image_tags: A high-resolution scan of the back of a weathered, light-blue or grey government document. The paper is aged with visible texture and two hole-punch marks at the top center. The primary text is faded, inverted at the bottom, and contains administrative stamps and handwritten notations in the margins.

elements.description: Page showing a faint, upside-down letterhead for 'MIDDLETOWN, PENNSYLVANIA' at the top. The body contains faint, illegible typed text. At the bottom, a clear typed stamp reads 'TRANSMITTED DEC 13 3 53 PM '48'. There are two punch holes at the top and faint handwritten markings in the top left corner.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual elements (aged paper, two punch holes, faint/imprint text, handwritten markings) without directly contradicting each other on quoted text, though B provides specific stamp text while A does not.
